Class NativeHandle

java.lang.Object
oshi.ffm.NativeHandle
All Implemented Interfaces:
AutoCloseable
public final class NativeHandle extends Object implements AutoCloseable
An AutoCloseable wrapper around a native handle (represented as a MemorySegment). When closed, the provided closer function is invoked to release the native resource.

This class is intended for use in try-with-resources blocks to ensure native handles are properly released, even when exceptions occur.

Example usage: 

try (NativeHandle h = NativeHandle.of(Kernel32FFM.OpenProcess(...), Kernel32FFM::CloseHandle)) {
    // use h.get()
}

    NativeHandle.ThrowingCloser
    A consumer that accepts a MemorySegment and may throw.

  • Method Details

    • of

      public static NativeHandle of(MemorySegment handle, NativeHandle.ThrowingCloser closer)
      Creates a new NativeHandle wrapping the given segment with the specified closer.
      Parameters:
      handle - the native handle segment; may be null or MemorySegment.NULL
      closer - the function to call to release the handle
      Returns:
      a new NativeHandle

    • get

      public MemorySegment get()
      Returns the underlying handle segment.
      Returns:
      the handle, which may be null or MemorySegment.NULL

    • isNull

      public boolean isNull()
      Tests whether this handle is null or points to address zero.
      Returns:
      true if the handle is null or MemorySegment.NULL

    • close

      public void close()
      Releases the native handle by invoking the closer. Does nothing if the handle is null or zero, or if already closed. Any exception thrown by the closer is logged and suppressed. This method is idempotent.
      Specified by:
      close in interface AutoCloseable
